approval_scope: Each item requires current-source re-review and a separate item-level decision. Approval to investigate is not approval to mutate D1.

items.finding: Stored $50 appears to be a registration fee, while the official Everett club page lists summer camp at $270/week full time or $180/week part time plus a $50 registration fee.
items.proposal: Remove the standalone $50 camp-price claim and re-review the exact Everett program, date scope, ages, and full price from the provider page before approval.

items.finding: Official page supports $245 and grades 3-8, but not the stored $196 early-bird qualifier or exact ages 8-14.
items.proposal: Remove the unsupported promotional qualifier; represent grade eligibility directly or label any age conversion approximate with provenance.

items.finding: Stored $119-$349 range reflects struck-through prices; the current official registration page displays early-bird prices of $99-$309 across program variants.
items.proposal: Refresh price text with explicit variant/discount context and recheck after the early-bird window; do not imply one product spans every age and price.

items.finding: Official Parks Tacoma page supports name, dates, and ages and currently shows waitlist, but the sampled registration page did not reproduce price.
items.proposal: Confirm the $250 price in a provider-controlled detail view and reconcile availability before the next release audit.
items.investigation_result: The current official camps and arts-camps pages reproduce Around the World Cooking Camp at STAR Center, Aug. 24-28, 2026, ages 13-17, with zero openings and waitlist status. They still do not reproduce the stored $250 price.

items.finding: Client-rendered Homeroom page did not expose enough public evidence to reproduce material fields.
items.proposal: Obtain a provider-controlled accessible detail page or manual evidence review; do not grant verified status from HTTP reachability.
items.investigation_result: A stable provider-controlled 2026 camp page now reproduces the overall Jun. 15-Aug. 7 season, rising grades 1-10, soccer as an offered sport, and price floors. It does not identify the exact sampled soccer session, dates, grade band, or price, so record-level material fields remain unverified.

items.finding: The i9 venue page did not expose public material fields in the audit client.
items.proposal: Reproduce dates, ages, price and early-bird deadline through an accessible official detail flow before verified status.

items.finding: The TeamSnap registration target was login/client-rendered and did not reproduce the stored Kirkland camp fields.
items.proposal: Require an official public program page or a documented manual provider review before verified status.

items.finding: The generic Puyallup catalog did not expose the sampled Week 9 material fields in the audit client.
items.proposal: Capture a stable provider detail URL or perform a dated manual review before verified status.
items.investigation_result: An official 2026 city scholarship form reproduces Camp Yougottawanna Week 9 as Color Wars, Aug. 17-21, at Puyallup Recreation Center. It does not reproduce the sampled record's ages or price, and the generic Rec1 catalog remains an unstable detail destination.

items.finding: The official Sound Life URL was not inspectable in the audit client, so dates, ages and price were not reproduced.
items.proposal: Reproduce through an accessible official page or dated manual provider evidence before verified status.
items.investigation_result: The queued Tacoma URL remains unverified. The currently discoverable provider-branded 2026 Sound Life site is explicitly for Port Angeles at 120 West 8th Street and therefore cannot support the Tacoma record. Treat this as a possible entity/location mismatch, not as substitute evidence.

items.finding: The official Auburn guide states a minimum age of 11.5 while the directory broadens it to age 11; the stored program identifier must be re-established before any record-level proposal.
items.proposal: Choose and document a half-year age representation rule. Until the schema can represent 11.5 accurately, do not label the transformed minimum as source-verified and do not mutate a record from this policy placeholder.
